title: gmail_send sends real email immediately despite its own docstring promising draft-only + Ash confirmation
source: Claude Fable audit 2026-07-04 (discovered while scoping T-257/T-258 Gmail-Telegram triage flow)
current_state: tools/tools_gmail.py module docstring (line 18): 'gmail_send(to, subject, body) -> draft-only by default (Ash must confirm)'. The method's own docstring (line 179, pre-fix): 'Send an email. Returns draft info - actual send requires Ash confirmation.' But the implementation called svc.users().messages().send(userId='me', body={'raw': encoded}).execute() directly - a REAL, IMMEDIATE send with no draft, no confirmation step, no human-in-the-loop gate of any kind. No drafts().create() call exists anywhere in the file (confirmed via grep). Any mode/tool-caller (root Claude, sprint.py, a misfired guest-approved action) invoking gmail_send believes - per its own contract - that it is creating a reviewable draft. It is actually sending real email on Ash's behalf immediately. This is a trust-boundary violation discovered while scoping T-258 (Telegram email-triage buttons), which explicitly planned to rely on gmail_send's documented draft-only behavior as its human-in-the-loop safety mechanism - building T-258 on the un-fixed code would have wired a real auto-send path by accident.
target_state: gmail_send creates a real Gmail draft via svc.users().drafts().create(userId='me', body={'message': {'raw': encoded}}).execute() and returns {'success', 'draft_id', 'message_id', 'to', 'subject'}. No code path in this method sends email immediately. Ash reviews and sends the draft from Gmail itself (or a future explicit gmail_send_draft(draft_id) confirmation tool, out of scope here). Docstrings now match implementation exactly.
migration_plan: 1. tools/tools_gmail.py gmail_send: replace 'svc.users().messages().send(userId="me", body={"raw": encoded}).execute()' with 'svc.users().drafts().create(userId="me", body={"message": {"raw": encoded}}).execute()'.
2. Update return dict to draft_id (and message_id from result.get('message', {}).get('id')) instead of a top-level send message_id.

3. Existing test testing/test_tools_gmail.py::test_gmail_send_graceful only checks the failure path (service unavailable) - unaffected. Add a new test asserting the success path calls drafts().create and NOT messages().send (mock the service, assert call recorded on the drafts mock, assert messages().send was never invoked).
4. python scripts/verify.py PASS; append solutions/SOLUTIONS.jsonl; close ticket.
5. Re-check T-258's plan against this fix: the 'Draft reply' button now genuinely creates a Gmail draft with zero auto-send risk.
risk_notes: This changes gmail_send's real-world behavior (was: sends immediately; now: creates draft only) to match what it always claimed to do. Any existing caller that relied on immediate delivery (none found in this codebase - grep shows no other gmail_send callers yet, since T-257/T-258 are the first) would need to switch to an explicit send-confirmation step, which does not exist yet and is intentionally out of scope for this ticket (draft creation is the safe default; a confirm-and-send tool is a separate, deliberate addition if ever needed).
